response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.
response_parsed.opinion:
I've been following the news on COVID vaccines because I'm a bit anxious about staying healthy, especially since my husband passed away from complications related to something similar years ago. But honestly, it can be overwhelming when people start talking online or in person without knowing what they're doing. My parents have strong opinions too—my dad has only a high school diploma but he's very into science fiction and that makes him curious about tech stuff like this vaccines. On the other hand, my mom actually worked as an RN for decades before retiring so she knows real medical stuff.

I try to manage these discussions by first asking questions because I'm naturally curious from my openness score being 96. But sometimes people just want arguments and that's exhausting when you're dealing with anxiety like me. So now, if someone starts talking at work or even in my social media feeds where I spend about an hour daily scrolling through stuff—it gets so confusing between what the doctors say versus all these internet rumors.

I also volunteer a bit which keeps me grounded but healthcare topics still make me nervous because of how much can go wrong without knowing enough details. Maybe suggesting everyone reads from reliable sources could help? Though I don't have time to read everything deeply since my conscientiousness is low and agreeableness even lower.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: I started with acknowledging personal background due to high openness trait, then explained how anxiety influences approach based on moderate neuroticism score. Addressed family dynamics by mentioning parents' education levels aligning with low agreeableness but high extraversion in the persona's ocean scores.
